title: Driver Frontal Stage 1 Deployment Control
shortDescription: Malfunction in the driver-side frontal airbag stage 1 deployment circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0001 indicates a fault in the driver frontal stage 1 airbag deployment control circuit. The airbag control module (ACM) has detected an abnormal condition in the squib circuit that fires the first stage of the driver frontal airbag. This could be an open circuit, high resistance, short to ground, or short to voltage condition. The airbag warning light will illuminate and the driver frontal airbag may not deploy in a collision.
safetyImpact: Driver frontal airbag may not deploy in a collision, significantly increasing risk of serious injury or death.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring in the airbag squib circuit

symptoms: Airbag warning light illuminated on dashboard
SRS (Supplemental Restraint System) warning message displayed
Diagnostic trouble code stored in airbag control module

fixSteps.title: Scan for codes
fixSteps.instruction: Connect an OBD-II scanner capable of reading SRS codes. Record all stored and pending codes. Clear codes and check if they return immediately.

fixSteps.title: Inspect clockspring
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the steering wheel airbag module carefully (disconnect battery first and wait 10 minutes). Inspect the clockspring connector and wiring for damage, corrosion, or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Test squib circuit resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter with SRS-safe leads, measure resistance across the driver airbag squib connector. Expected resistance is typically 2-4 ohms. Also check for shorts to ground and shorts to voltage.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Based on test results, replace the clockspring, airbag module, or wiring harness as needed. After replacement, clear codes and verify repair by test driving.

faq.q: Is it safe to drive with code B0001?
faq.a: The vehicle is mechanically drivable, but the driver airbag may not deploy in a crash. Repair should be treated as urgent for safety reasons.

faq.q: Can I reset the airbag light without fixing the problem?
faq.a: Clearing the code will temporarily turn off the light, but it will return if the underlying fault persists. The airbag system will remain non-functional until properly repaired.

faq.q: Why is this repair so expensive?
faq.a: Airbag components are safety-critical and must meet strict OEM specifications. The clockspring and airbag module are relatively expensive parts, and labor involves careful handling of explosive squib devices.

title: Driver Frontal Stage 2 Deployment Control
shortDescription: Fault in the driver frontal airbag stage 2 deployment circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0002 indicates a problem with the second stage deployment circuit for the driver frontal airbag. Modern dual-stage airbags use a two-stage inflator to modulate deployment force based on crash severity. Stage 2 provides additional inflation for higher-severity impacts. This code means the ACM cannot properly control the second stage, which may result in reduced airbag inflation during a severe collision.
safetyImpact: Driver airbag may only deploy at reduced force during severe collisions, potentially reducing occupant protection.

causes.cause: Damaged wiring harness to stage 2 squib
Corroded connector pins at airbag module

fixSteps.title: Read and document codes
fixSteps.instruction: Use an SRS-capable scanner to read all airbag-related codes. Document freeze frame data if available.

fixSteps.title: Inspect airbag module connector
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect battery, wait 10 minutes. Remove driver airbag module and inspect the electrical connector for corrosion, bent pins, or damage.

fixSteps.title: Test stage 2 squib circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance on the stage 2 circuit pins. Compare with manufacturer specification (typically 2-4 ohms). Check for continuity to ground (should be open).

fixSteps.title: Replace defective component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the airbag module or repair wiring as indicated by testing. Clear codes and confirm repair.

faq.q: What is a dual-stage airbag?
faq.a: A dual-stage airbag uses two separate inflator charges. Stage 1 fires for moderate impacts and stage 2 fires additionally for severe impacts, providing more inflation force when needed.

faq.q: Will my airbag still work with this code?
faq.a: Stage 1 may still function, but stage 2 will not. This means the airbag may deploy with reduced force in a severe crash, offering less protection.

faq.q: Can I replace the airbag module myself?
faq.a: Airbag replacement involves handling explosive components and is generally recommended for professional technicians only. Improper handling can cause accidental deployment.

title: Passenger Frontal Stage 1 Deployment Control
shortDescription: Malfunction in the passenger-side frontal airbag stage 1 deployment circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0003 signals a fault in the passenger frontal airbag stage 1 deployment control circuit. The airbag control module has detected a problem with the passenger-side squib firing circuit that could prevent the passenger airbag from deploying during a frontal collision. The issue may be electrical (open, short, high resistance) within the circuit between the ACM and the passenger airbag module located in the dashboard.
safetyImpact: Passenger frontal airbag may fail to deploy, putting the front-seat passenger at serious risk in a collision.
symptoms: Passenger airbag OFF indicator may illuminate unexpectedly
SRS system fault message on instrument cluster

fixSteps.title: Diagnostic scan
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a full SRS system scan. Note all current and history codes. Check if the passenger airbag disable switch is in the correct position.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring and connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect battery and wait 10 minutes. Access the passenger airbag module connectors behind the dashboard. Check for damaged, pinched, or corroded wiring.

fixSteps.title: Measure circuit resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Using SRS-safe test leads, measure resistance across the stage 1 squib circuit. Verify readings match manufacturer specs. Check for shorts to ground and battery voltage.

fixSteps.title: Repair or replace
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the passenger airbag module, repair wiring, or replace connectors as indicated. Clear codes and verify the airbag light turns off after a drive cycle.

faq.q: Could this code be caused by something spilled on the dashboard?
faq.a: Yes, liquid spills can seep into the dashboard area and corrode airbag connectors or damage wiring, triggering this fault.

faq.q: Is the passenger airbag OFF indicator related?
faq.a: It can be. Some vehicles disable the passenger airbag entirely when a fault is detected in the deployment circuit, illuminating the OFF indicator.

faq.q: How much does a passenger airbag module cost?
faq.a: OEM passenger airbag modules typically range from $200 to $600 depending on the vehicle. Aftermarket options may be available at lower cost but must meet safety standards.

title: Passenger Frontal Stage 2 Deployment Control
shortDescription: Fault in the passenger frontal airbag stage 2 deployment circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0004 indicates a malfunction in the second-stage deployment circuit of the passenger frontal airbag. Similar to the driver side, the passenger airbag uses a dual-stage inflator. This code means the stage 2 circuit has an electrical fault preventing the ACM from firing the second inflation stage during a severe impact. Stage 1 may still function, but overall protection is compromised.
safetyImpact: Passenger airbag may deploy with insufficient force in severe collisions, reducing occupant protection.
symptoms: SRS fault stored in control module

fixSteps.title: Retrieve diagnostic data
fixSteps.instruction: Read all SRS codes and freeze frame data. Determine whether the fault is current or historical.

fixSteps.title: Visual inspection
fixSteps.instruction: After disconnecting the battery and waiting 10 minutes, inspect the passenger airbag module connector and associated wiring for physical damage.

fixSteps.title: Electrical testing
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the stage 2 squib circuit resistance and check for shorts. Compare readings against manufacturer specifications.

fixSteps.title: Component replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty airbag module or repair wiring as needed. Clear codes and perform a verification drive cycle.

faq.q: What happens if only stage 1 deploys?
faq.a: In a moderate crash, stage 1 alone provides adequate protection. However, in a severe crash requiring full inflation force, the airbag may not cushion the occupant sufficiently.

faq.q: Can aftermarket parts be used for this repair?
faq.a: Airbag modules should meet Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards. OEM parts are recommended. If using aftermarket, ensure they are certified and compatible.

faq.q: Will my vehicle pass inspection with this code?
faq.a: In most jurisdictions, an illuminated airbag light will cause a safety inspection failure.

title: Driver Side (Curtain/Torso) Airbag Deployment Control
shortDescription: Fault in the driver-side curtain or torso airbag deployment circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0005 indicates a malfunction in the driver-side curtain or torso airbag deployment control circuit. This airbag is designed to protect the driver during side-impact collisions and rollovers. The ACM has detected an electrical anomaly in the side airbag squib circuit, which could prevent deployment when needed. This may involve the side curtain airbag in the headliner or the torso airbag in the seat.
safetyImpact: Driver side-impact protection is compromised. The curtain or torso airbag may not deploy in a side collision or rollover.

causes.cause: Damaged wiring in the driver seat or B-pillar area

symptoms: Code stored in airbag control module

fixSteps.title: Full system scan
fixSteps.instruction: Scan the SRS system for all codes. Side airbag faults often accompany other airbag codes, so review all stored DTCs.

fixSteps.title: Inspect seat-side wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect battery, wait 10 minutes. Check the wiring harness running through the driver seat, particularly at the connector under the seat and along the B-pillar.

fixSteps.title: Test squib resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the side airbag squib circuit resistance at the ACM connector. Typical resistance is 2-5 ohms. Check for opens and shorts.

fixSteps.title: Replace component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the side airbag module or repair wiring. Clear codes and verify the warning light stays off.

faq.q: Where is the driver side airbag located?
faq.a: Depending on the vehicle, it may be in the driver seat bolster (torso airbag) or in the roof headliner along the driver side (curtain airbag), or both.

faq.q: Can seat adjustments cause this code?
faq.a: Yes. Frequent seat movement can wear the wiring harness connector under the seat, leading to intermittent or permanent faults in the side airbag circuit.

faq.q: Is the side airbag as important as the frontal airbag?
faq.a: Side airbags are critical for side-impact protection and rollovers. Side impacts account for a significant percentage of serious collision injuries.

title: Passenger Side (Curtain/Torso) Airbag Deployment Control
shortDescription: Fault in the passenger-side curtain or torso airbag deployment circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0010 indicates a fault in the passenger-side curtain or side torso airbag deployment circuit. The airbag control module has detected an electrical problem that could prevent the passenger side airbag from deploying during a side impact or rollover event. This covers the side curtain and/or torso airbag on the passenger side of the vehicle.
safetyImpact: Passenger side-impact protection is reduced. Side airbag may not deploy during a lateral collision.

causes.cause: Wiring damage in passenger seat or C-pillar area

fixSteps.title: Scan SRS system
fixSteps.instruction: Retrieve all stored SRS codes. Check for companion codes that may indicate a broader wiring problem.

fixSteps.title: Inspect passenger seat wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect battery, wait 10 minutes. Inspect the wiring under the passenger seat and along the B/C-pillar for damage, pinching, or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Measure circuit resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Test the passenger side airbag squib circuit for proper resistance, opens, and shorts using SRS-safe equipment.

fixSteps.title: Replace or repair
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the defective side airbag module or repair wiring. Clear codes and confirm the light remains off after driving.

faq.q: Can items stored under the passenger seat cause this code?
faq.a: Yes. Items pushed under the seat can pinch or damage the airbag wiring harness, causing intermittent or permanent faults.

faq.q: Does this affect the passenger frontal airbag?
faq.a: No. This code is specific to the side airbag. However, if the SRS system is fully disabled due to this fault, other airbags may also be affected depending on the vehicle.

faq.q: How do I know if my car has side airbags?
faq.a: Check for SRS or AIRBAG labels on the seat bolsters, headliner edges, or B-pillars. Your owner's manual will also list all airbag locations.

title: Driver Knee Airbag Deployment Control
shortDescription: Fault in the driver knee airbag deployment circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0015 indicates a malfunction in the driver knee airbag deployment control circuit. The knee airbag is located in the lower dashboard area and deploys downward to protect the driver's knees and lower legs during a frontal collision, preventing the driver from submarining under the dashboard. The ACM has detected an electrical fault in this circuit.
safetyImpact: Driver knee protection compromised. Lower extremity injuries more likely in a frontal collision.

causes.cause: Damaged wiring behind lower dashboard panel
Loose connector at knee airbag module
ACM fault affecting knee airbag channel

fixSteps.title: Read codes
fixSteps.instruction: Scan SRS module for all codes. Check if the knee airbag code is current or history.

fixSteps.title: Access knee airbag
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect battery, wait 10 minutes. Remove the lower dashboard trim panels to access the knee airbag module and its connector.

fixSteps.title: Test circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Measure squib resistance and check for opens, shorts to ground, and shorts to voltage on the knee airbag circuit.

fixSteps.title: Replace or repair
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the knee airbag module or repair wiring as needed. Reassemble trim and clear codes.

faq.q: What does the knee airbag protect?
faq.a: The knee airbag protects the driver's knees and lower legs in a frontal crash, and helps maintain proper positioning relative to the frontal airbag.

faq.q: Is the knee airbag essential?
faq.a: While the frontal airbag is the primary safety device, the knee airbag provides important supplemental protection and is part of the overall restraint system design.

faq.q: Can aftermarket floor mats interfere with the knee airbag?
faq.a: Improperly fitted floor mats can potentially interfere with knee airbag deployment. Always use mats designed for your specific vehicle.

title: Passenger Knee Airbag Deployment Control
shortDescription: Fault in the passenger knee airbag deployment circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0020 indicates a fault in the passenger knee airbag deployment control circuit. This airbag, located in the lower glove box area or lower dashboard on the passenger side, protects the front passenger's knees and lower legs. The ACM has detected an electrical anomaly in this deployment circuit.
safetyImpact: Passenger knee protection is compromised during frontal collisions.

causes.cause: Wiring damage behind glove box or lower dash

fixSteps.title: Diagnostic scan
fixSteps.instruction: Read all SRS codes from the ACM. Check for multiple codes that might indicate a shared wiring issue.

fixSteps.title: Access and inspect
fixSteps.instruction: Remove glove box and lower dash trim to access the passenger knee airbag. Inspect the connector and wiring for damage.

fixSteps.title: Electrical test
fixSteps.instruction: Measure squib circuit resistance and check for faults. Compare with manufacturer specifications.

fixSteps.title: Repair
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the knee airbag module or repair wiring. Clear codes and verify the repair.

faq.q: Do all vehicles have passenger knee airbags?
faq.a: No. Passenger knee airbags became more common after 2010 but are not universal. Check your owner's manual for your vehicle's specific airbag configuration.

faq.q: Can glove box items cause this code?
faq.a: Overstuffing the glove box does not typically cause this code, but physical damage to the area behind the glove box during repairs or modifications could affect the wiring.

faq.q: Is this code related to the passenger occupant sensor?
faq.a: No. This code is specific to the knee airbag deployment circuit. The occupant sensor is a separate system with its own codes.

subcategory: Driver Seat Belt Pretensioner Deployment Control
title: Driver Seat Belt Pretensioner Deployment Control
shortDescription: Malfunction in the driver seat belt pretensioner deployment circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0024 indicates a fault in the driver-side seat belt pretensioner deployment circuit. The seat belt pretensioner uses a pyrotechnic charge to instantly tighten the seat belt during a collision, removing slack to hold the occupant firmly in position before the airbag deploys. This code means the ACM cannot fire the pretensioner, reducing restraint effectiveness.
safetyImpact: Driver seat belt will not pre-tension during a collision, allowing more forward movement and potentially reducing airbag effectiveness.

causes.cause: Damaged pretensioner wiring in B-pillar area

symptoms: Seat belt appears to function normally when buckling

fixSteps.title: Scan for codes
fixSteps.instruction: Read all SRS codes. The pretensioner code may be accompanied by related airbag deployment codes.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pretensioner wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect battery, wait 10 minutes. Remove the B-pillar trim to access the pretensioner connector. Inspect wiring for damage or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance on the pretensioner squib circuit at the ACM connector. Verify against specifications. Check for opens and shorts.

fixSteps.title: Replace pretensioner
fixSteps.instruction: If the pretensioner unit is faulty, replace the complete seat belt assembly as the pretensioner is not separately serviceable. Clear codes.

faq.q: What does a seat belt pretensioner do?
faq.a: During a crash, the pretensioner fires a pyrotechnic charge that instantly retracts the belt webbing, removing slack and holding you firmly in position before the airbag deploys.

faq.q: Does my seat belt still work without the pretensioner?
faq.a: The seat belt still locks and restrains you through the normal inertia locking mechanism. However, without pretensioning, there will be more slack and forward movement during a crash.

faq.q: Can the pretensioner be repaired, or must it be replaced?
faq.a: Pretensioners are one-time-use pyrotechnic devices. If faulty, the entire seat belt retractor assembly must be replaced.

subcategory: Passenger Seat Belt Pretensioner Deployment Control
title: Passenger Seat Belt Pretensioner Deployment Control
shortDescription: Malfunction in the passenger seat belt pretensioner deployment circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0028 indicates a fault in the passenger seat belt pretensioner deployment control circuit. The ACM has detected an electrical problem preventing it from firing the passenger seat belt pretensioner during a collision event. This reduces the effectiveness of the passenger restraint system.
safetyImpact: Passenger seat belt pretensioner will not fire during a collision, allowing more forward movement of the occupant.
symptoms: Seat belt operates normally for buckling

fixSteps.title: Read codes
fixSteps.instruction: Scan the SRS system for all codes. Document which circuits are affected.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect battery, wait 10 minutes. Access the passenger B-pillar area and inspect pretensioner wiring and connector.

fixSteps.title: Circuit testing
fixSteps.instruction: Test the pretensioner squib circuit for proper resistance and check for shorts. Compare with factory specifications.

fixSteps.title: Replace assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the passenger seat belt assembly if the pretensioner is faulty. Clear codes and verify the repair.

faq.q: Are pretensioners reusable after a crash?
faq.a: No. Pretensioners are one-time-use pyrotechnic devices. After firing in a crash, the entire seat belt assembly must be replaced.

faq.q: Will my insurance cover pretensioner replacement?
faq.a: If the replacement is due to a collision, it should be covered under your collision claim. If it is a pre-existing electrical fault, it may be a warranty or out-of-pocket expense.

faq.q: Can a pretensioner fire accidentally?
faq.a: Under normal conditions, no. The pretensioner requires a specific signal from the ACM to fire. However, improper handling during repairs can cause accidental deployment.

title: Deployment Commanded - Frontal Crash
shortDescription: The airbag control module recorded that a deployment was commanded due to a frontal crash event.
fullDescription: Code B0051 is a post-crash informational code indicating that the airbag control module commanded deployment of one or more restraint devices during a frontal crash event. This code is stored in the ACM event data recorder after an airbag deployment. It confirms the system operated as intended and does not indicate a malfunction. The ACM must be replaced after deployment.
safetyImpact: This is a post-crash record. The vehicle should not be driven until all deployed restraints are replaced and the vehicle is deemed safe.

causes.cause: ACM commanded deployment based on crash algorithm

symptoms: Vehicle likely has significant collision damage

fixSteps.title: Assess vehicle damage
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a thorough assessment of all collision damage. Determine if the vehicle is repairable or a total loss.

fixSteps.title: Replace deployed components
fixSteps.instruction: Replace all deployed airbag modules, seat belt pretensioners, and the airbag control module. These are single-use components.

fixSteps.title: Replace ACM
fixSteps.instruction: The airbag control module must be replaced after a deployment event. It cannot be reset or reused. Program the new ACM to the vehicle.

fixSteps.title: Full system verification
fixSteps.instruction: After all components are replaced, perform a complete SRS system scan to verify no codes remain. Test drive to confirm all lights are off.

faq.q: Does this code mean there is a malfunction?
faq.a: No. This code confirms that the airbag system deployed as designed during a collision. It is an event record, not a fault code.

faq.q: Can the airbag control module be reused after deployment?
faq.a: No. The ACM must be replaced after an airbag deployment event. It stores crash data that cannot be cleared.

faq.q: Is the vehicle safe to drive after airbag deployment?
faq.a: No. The vehicle should not be driven until all deployed safety components are replaced and the SRS system is fully restored.

title: Deployment Commanded - Side or Rollover Crash
shortDescription: Airbag deployment was commanded during a side impact or rollover event.
fullDescription: Code B0060 is a post-crash event record indicating the ACM commanded deployment of side curtain, side torso, or rollover protection devices during a side impact or rollover crash. This code confirms proper system operation and is stored in the ACM event data recorder. All deployed components and the ACM must be replaced.
safetyImpact: Post-crash record. Vehicle must not be driven until all safety systems are restored.

causes.cause: Vehicle rollover detected by rollover sensor
ACM deployment algorithm triggered by lateral impact data

symptoms: Significant side or rollover collision damage

fixSteps.title: Damage assessment
fixSteps.instruction: Assess all side impact and rollover damage. Check structural integrity of the vehicle.

fixSteps.title: Replace all deployed restraints
fixSteps.instruction: Replace all deployed side airbags, curtain airbags, pretensioners, and any damaged sensors.

fixSteps.title: Replace ACM and sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the airbag control module and any side-impact or rollover sensors that were involved. Program the new ACM.

fixSteps.title: System verification
fixSteps.instruction: Perform complete SRS system scan to verify all codes clear and the system is fully operational.

faq.q: What is a rollover sensor?
faq.a: A rollover sensor detects the rate and angle of vehicle roll. When a rollover is imminent, it triggers side curtain airbags to deploy and stay inflated longer than in a side impact.

faq.q: Can side curtain airbags be replaced separately?
faq.a: Yes. Side curtain airbags are separate modules mounted in the headliner and can be replaced individually.

faq.q: Why is this repair so expensive?
faq.a: Rollover and side impact events often deploy multiple airbags simultaneously, and each must be replaced along with the ACM and potentially structural components.

title: Driver Front Impact Sensor Circuit
shortDescription: Fault in the driver-side front impact acceleration sensor circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0081 indicates a malfunction in the driver-side front impact sensor (satellite sensor). This sensor is mounted in the front structure of the vehicle and provides supplemental crash data to the ACM for frontal impact detection. A fault in this sensor circuit can prevent proper crash detection and airbag deployment timing.
safetyImpact: Frontal crash detection capability is reduced, potentially delaying or preventing airbag deployment.

causes.cause: Damaged front impact sensor from minor collision
Corroded sensor connector due to water intrusion
Wiring damage in the front structural area

fixSteps.title: Scan codes
fixSteps.instruction: Read all SRS codes. Front impact sensor codes may indicate prior minor collision damage.

fixSteps.title: Locate and inspect sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The front impact sensor is typically mounted behind the front bumper fascia on the frame rail. Inspect for physical damage, corrosion, or water intrusion.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Check wiring continuity from the sensor to the ACM. Measure sensor connector voltage and signal integrity.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the front impact sensor if damaged or faulty. Ensure proper mounting orientation. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: Can a minor fender bender damage the front impact sensor?
faq.a: Yes. Even minor frontal impacts can damage or misalign the front impact sensors, triggering this code.

faq.q: Where exactly is the front impact sensor?
faq.a: It is typically located behind the front bumper fascia, mounted on the radiator support or front frame rail, one on each side.

faq.q: Can I drive with a faulty front impact sensor?
faq.a: The vehicle is drivable, but the airbag system may not detect frontal crashes properly. Repair should be prioritized for safety.

title: Passenger Front Impact Sensor Circuit
shortDescription: Fault in the passenger-side front impact acceleration sensor circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0084 indicates a malfunction in the passenger-side front impact satellite sensor circuit. This sensor provides the ACM with deceleration data from the passenger side of the front structure. Without this data, the ACM may not accurately assess crash severity from the passenger side, potentially affecting deployment decisions.
safetyImpact: Passenger-side frontal crash detection may be impaired, affecting airbag deployment timing and decisions.

causes.cause: Wiring damage in front passenger area

symptoms: Reduced crash sensing on passenger side

fixSteps.title: Read codes
fixSteps.instruction: Scan SRS system and note all codes. Check for evidence of prior collision repair.

fixSteps.title: Inspect sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the passenger-side front impact sensor behind the bumper fascia. Check for damage, corrosion, and proper mounting.

fixSteps.title: Test wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check continuity and resistance from the sensor to the ACM. Verify power supply and signal output.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the sensor with OEM equivalent. Ensure correct orientation and torque. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: Could a previous accident repair have caused this code?
faq.a: Yes. If the sensor was not properly replaced or reconnected during a prior collision repair, this code may appear later.

faq.q: What happens if both front impact sensors fail?
faq.a: The ACM relies on its internal sensor and the front satellites for crash detection. Loss of satellite sensors reduces the system's ability to detect and characterize frontal impacts.

faq.q: Is the sensor directional?
faq.a: Yes. Front impact sensors must be mounted in the correct orientation. Installing one backward or rotated will cause incorrect readings.

title: Driver Side Impact Sensor Circuit
shortDescription: Fault in the driver-side lateral impact acceleration sensor circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0092 indicates a malfunction in the driver-side lateral impact sensor. This satellite sensor is mounted in the door or B-pillar and detects side-impact deceleration forces. The ACM uses this data to determine when to deploy side airbags. A fault prevents proper side-impact detection on the driver side.
safetyImpact: Driver-side lateral impact detection is compromised, potentially preventing side airbag deployment.

causes.cause: Door-mounted sensor damaged by door impact
Wiring damage in door jamb area
Corroded connector from water intrusion in door

fixSteps.title: Scan codes
fixSteps.instruction: Read all SRS codes and check for companion side-impact codes.

fixSteps.title: Inspect sensor and wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the driver door panel to access the side impact sensor. Check the sensor, connector, and wiring for damage, especially in the door jamb flex area.

fixSteps.title: Test circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Check continuity and resistance from the side impact sensor through the door wiring harness to the ACM. Pay attention to the door jamb boot area where wiring flexes.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor or repair wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty sensor or repair damaged wiring in the door jamb. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: Can a door slam damage the side impact sensor?
faq.a: Normal door operation should not damage the sensor. However, the wiring that passes through the door jamb can wear over time from repeated door opening and closing.

faq.q: Where is the side impact sensor located?
faq.a: It is typically mounted inside the door panel, on the door frame, or on the B-pillar. The exact location varies by manufacturer.

faq.q: Does this affect my frontal airbags?
faq.a: Not directly. However, on some vehicles, a fault in one SRS sensor can disable the entire airbag system as a precaution.

title: Passenger Side Impact Sensor Circuit
shortDescription: Fault in the passenger-side lateral impact acceleration sensor circuit.
fullDescription: Code B0095 indicates a malfunction in the passenger-side lateral impact sensor circuit. This sensor detects side-impact forces on the passenger side and communicates with the ACM to trigger side airbag deployment. A circuit fault can prevent proper crash detection and deployment on the passenger side.
safetyImpact: Passenger-side lateral impact detection impaired, potentially preventing side airbag deployment.

causes.cause: Passenger door impact damage to sensor
Door jamb wiring harness wear or damage
Water intrusion into door cavity corroding connector

fixSteps.title: Read codes
fixSteps.instruction: Scan SRS system. Check for multiple side-impact sensor codes.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Remove passenger door panel. Inspect the side impact sensor, connector, and wiring through the door jamb.

fixSteps.title: Test circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Check continuity from the sensor to the ACM. Test for opens, shorts, and proper resistance.

fixSteps.title: Repair
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the sensor or repair damaged wiring as needed. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: Can window tinting installation affect this sensor?
faq.a: Generally no, but if the installer damages door panel wiring during removal and reinstallation, it could affect the sensor circuit.

faq.q: Do I need a special tool to replace the sensor?
faq.a: Most side impact sensors are secured with standard bolts or screws. No special tools are typically needed beyond basic door panel removal tools.

faq.q: Will this code cause the car to fail emissions testing?
faq.a: No. SRS codes do not affect emissions testing. However, the airbag light may cause a safety inspection failure in some jurisdictions.

title: Electronic Frontal Sensor Module
shortDescription: Malfunction in the electronic frontal sensor module communication or performance.
fullDescription: Code B0100 indicates a problem with the electronic frontal sensor module. This module is part of the supplemental restraint system and provides critical crash severity data to the ACM. The fault may be internal to the sensor module or related to its communication link with the ACM. Without proper data from this module, the SRS system cannot accurately determine crash severity.
safetyImpact: Crash severity determination is compromised, potentially affecting airbag deployment decisions.

causes.cause: Communication bus fault between sensor and ACM
Wiring harness damage to sensor module

symptoms: Multiple SRS codes may be present

fixSteps.title: Full SRS scan
fixSteps.instruction: Read all SRS codes and check for communication-related codes that may indicate a bus fault.

fixSteps.title: Inspect module and wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the frontal sensor module (typically behind the front bumper on the radiator support) and inspect for damage, water intrusion, and connector condition.

fixSteps.title: Check communication
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the communication bus between the sensor module and ACM using a diagnostic scanner. Check for signal integrity.

fixSteps.title: Replace module
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the frontal sensor module if faulty. Some modules require programming to the vehicle. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: What is the difference between the frontal sensor module and front impact sensors?
faq.a: The electronic frontal sensor module is a more advanced sensor that may combine multiple sensing capabilities, while individual front impact sensors typically measure only acceleration in one direction.

faq.q: Can a minor front-end collision trigger this code?
faq.a: Yes. Even minor impacts can damage the frontal sensor module due to its location behind the front bumper.

faq.q: Does this module need to be programmed after replacement?
faq.a: On many vehicles, yes. The replacement module may need to be configured to the vehicle using a manufacturer-specific diagnostic tool.

title: SRS Backup Power Supply
shortDescription: Fault in the SRS backup power supply (energy reserve module).
fullDescription: Code B0110 indicates a malfunction in the SRS backup power supply, also known as the energy reserve module. This component stores electrical energy so the airbags can still deploy even if the vehicle battery is disconnected during a collision. A fault means the SRS system may not have backup power if the main battery supply is lost during a crash.
safetyImpact: If the vehicle battery connection is severed during a crash, the airbags may not deploy due to lack of backup power.

causes.cause: Degraded backup capacitor in energy reserve module
Low vehicle battery voltage affecting charging
Faulty wiring to energy reserve module
ACM internal backup power circuit failure

symptoms: May occur intermittently with low battery

fixSteps.title: Check battery condition
fixSteps.instruction: Test the vehicle battery voltage and charging system output. Low voltage can trigger this code. Battery should read 12.4V+ at rest and 13.5-14.5V running.

fixSteps.title: Scan and clear codes
fixSteps.instruction: If the battery was recently replaced or disconnected, clear codes and monitor. The code may resolve once the backup capacitor is fully charged.

fixSteps.title: Test energy reserve circuit
fixSteps.instruction: If the code returns after charging, test the energy reserve module circuit for proper voltage and capacitor charge.

fixSteps.title: Replace module
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the energy reserve module or ACM if the internal backup power circuit has failed. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: Can a weak battery cause this code?
faq.a: Yes. A weak or undercharged battery can prevent the backup energy reserve from charging properly, triggering this code.

faq.q: Will replacing the battery fix this code?
faq.a: If the code was caused by low battery voltage, replacing or charging the battery and clearing the code may resolve it. If the energy reserve module itself is faulty, it will need replacement.

faq.q: What is the energy reserve module?
faq.a: It is a capacitor-based device that stores enough electrical energy to deploy the airbags even if the vehicle battery is disconnected during the initial milliseconds of a crash.

title: Airbag Control Module Internal Fault
shortDescription: Internal malfunction detected within the airbag control module.
fullDescription: Code B0130 indicates an internal fault within the airbag control module (ACM). The module has detected an error in its own internal circuitry, memory, or processing that prevents it from functioning reliably. This is a critical fault because the ACM is the central brain of the entire supplemental restraint system. An internal ACM fault typically requires module replacement.
safetyImpact: The entire SRS system may be non-functional. No airbags or pretensioners may deploy in a collision.

causes.cause: Water or moisture damage to ACM
Power supply voltage spike or reverse polarity

fixSteps.title: Verify the code
fixSteps.instruction: Scan SRS system with a professional-grade scanner. An internal ACM fault often means the module cannot communicate properly. Try clearing codes first.

fixSteps.title: Check ACM environment
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the ACM for signs of water damage, corrosion, or physical damage. The ACM is typically located under the center console or under the driver/passenger seat.

fixSteps.title: Check power and ground
fixSteps.instruction: Verify proper battery voltage supply and ground connections to the ACM. Poor grounds can cause internal errors.

fixSteps.title: Replace ACM
fixSteps.instruction: If the internal fault persists, replace the ACM with a new or properly remanufactured unit. Program it to the vehicle and clear all codes.

faq.q: Can the ACM be repaired instead of replaced?
faq.a: Some companies offer ACM repair/rebuild services that can be significantly cheaper than a new unit. However, ensure the service is reputable and the rebuilt unit meets OEM specifications.

faq.q: Does the new ACM need to be programmed?
faq.a: Yes. The replacement ACM must be programmed or configured to the specific vehicle using a manufacturer diagnostic tool or compatible programmer.

faq.q: Can a jump-start cause ACM failure?
faq.a: Improper jump-starting (reverse polarity) can damage the ACM and other electronic modules. Always follow proper jump-start procedures.

title: Occupant Classification System Malfunction
shortDescription: Fault in the passenger seat occupant classification system.
fullDescription: Code B0148 indicates a malfunction in the occupant classification system (OCS), which determines whether the front passenger seat is occupied and classifies the occupant (adult, child, empty) to decide whether to enable or suppress the passenger airbag. A fault can cause the airbag to be improperly enabled or suppressed, posing a safety risk.
safetyImpact: Passenger airbag may deploy when it should be suppressed (child in seat) or may not deploy when needed (adult occupant).

causes.cause: Faulty weight sensor in passenger seat cushion

symptoms: Passenger airbag OFF indicator behaving erratically
Passenger airbag OFF light stays on with adult seated
Passenger airbag ON with empty seat

fixSteps.title: Scan codes
fixSteps.instruction: Read all SRS codes. Check the passenger airbag status indicator on the dashboard with different loads on the seat.

fixSteps.title: Test OCS system
fixSteps.instruction: Using a scanner with bidirectional capability, check the OCS sensor readings with the seat empty, with a known weight, and with an adult seated. Compare readings to specs.

fixSteps.title: Inspect seat sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If readings are abnormal, the seat sensor mat or bladder may need replacement. This requires removing the seat cushion cover.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor or module
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty OCS sensor mat or classification module. Recalibrate the system using the manufacturer procedure. Clear codes.

faq.q: Why does my passenger airbag OFF light stay on with an adult in the seat?
faq.a: The occupant classification sensor may be misreading the occupant's weight due to a faulty sensor mat, or the occupant may be below the weight threshold. Try having the passenger sit centered and upright.

faq.q: Can seat covers affect the occupant classification system?
faq.a: Yes. Thick seat covers can interfere with the weight/pressure sensor in the seat cushion, causing incorrect classification of the occupant.

faq.q: Does the OCS system need recalibration after seat work?
faq.a: In many cases, yes. Any work on the seat cushion or frame may require OCS recalibration using a manufacturer-specific procedure.

title: Body Control Module (BCM) Internal Fault
shortDescription: Internal malfunction within the body control module.
fullDescription: Code B1000 indicates an internal fault within the Body Control Module (BCM). The BCM manages numerous body electrical functions including lighting, door locks, windows, wipers, and other convenience features. An internal fault can affect multiple vehicle systems and may cause intermittent or permanent failures of BCM-controlled functions.
safetyImpact: May affect critical body functions such as exterior lighting, turn signals, or windshield wipers, reducing visibility and safety.

causes.cause: Power supply irregularity or voltage spike

symptoms: Dashboard warning lights for multiple systems
Wipers or turn signals operating erratically

fixSteps.title: Comprehensive scan
fixSteps.instruction: Scan all vehicle modules for codes. BCM faults often trigger secondary codes in other systems.

fixSteps.title: Check power and grounds
fixSteps.instruction: Verify battery voltage, BCM power supply pins, and all BCM ground connections. Clean any corroded ground points.

fixSteps.title: Inspect BCM for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the BCM (usually behind the dashboard or in the kick panel area). Inspect for water damage, corrosion, or burnt components.

fixSteps.title: Replace and program BCM
fixSteps.instruction: If the BCM has an internal fault, replace it with a new or remanufactured unit. The BCM must be programmed to the vehicle with all options configured.

faq.q: What functions does the BCM control?
faq.a: The BCM typically controls exterior and interior lighting, door locks, windows, wipers, horn, turn signals, security system, and various convenience features. The exact functions vary by vehicle.

faq.q: Can a BCM be reflashed instead of replaced?
faq.a: Sometimes. If the issue is software-related, a BCM reflash or update using the manufacturer tool may resolve the problem without hardware replacement.

faq.q: Will a used BCM work as a replacement?
faq.a: Used BCMs often require programming to match the specific vehicle. Some can be reprogrammed, while others are vehicle-specific and cannot be transferred.

title: BCM Option Configuration Error
shortDescription: Body control module has an option configuration mismatch or error.
fullDescription: Code B1001 indicates that the Body Control Module has detected an option configuration error. This means the BCM's programmed configuration does not match the vehicle's actual equipment or the configuration data has become corrupted. This can occur after a BCM replacement, software update, or battery disconnection event.
safetyImpact: Vehicle features may not function correctly. Safety-related items like lighting or locks could be affected.

causes.cause: BCM not properly configured after replacement

fixSteps.title: Scan all modules
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a full vehicle scan. Check for codes in all modules that may indicate configuration mismatches.

fixSteps.title: Verify BCM configuration
fixSteps.instruction: Using a manufacturer-level diagnostic tool, read the BCM option configuration and compare it against the vehicle's actual equipment (RPO codes or build sheet).

fixSteps.title: Reconfigure BCM
fixSteps.instruction: Program the correct option configuration into the BCM using the setup/configuration procedure for the specific vehicle.

fixSteps.title: Verify all functions
fixSteps.instruction: After reconfiguration, test all BCM-controlled functions (lights, locks, windows, wipers, etc.) to verify correct operation. Clear codes.

faq.q: What are RPO codes?
faq.a: Regular Production Option codes identify the specific equipment and options installed on your vehicle from the factory. They are typically found on a sticker in the glove box or trunk.

faq.q: Can I configure the BCM myself?
faq.a: BCM configuration typically requires a manufacturer-level or professional diagnostic tool with programming capability. Consumer-grade scanners usually cannot perform this function.

faq.q: Will disconnecting the battery cause this code?
faq.a: In most vehicles, battery disconnection will not erase BCM configuration. However, some vehicles may require a relearn procedure after battery reconnection.

title: BCM Power Supply Voltage Low
shortDescription: Body control module is receiving insufficient voltage from the vehicle power supply.
fullDescription: Code B1004 indicates that the Body Control Module is detecting a lower-than-acceptable voltage on its power supply circuit. The BCM requires stable voltage (typically above 9V minimum) to function properly. Low voltage can cause erratic BCM behavior and multiple body electrical symptoms.
safetyImpact: Low voltage can cause erratic operation of lights, wipers, and other safety-related body functions.

causes.cause: Corroded battery terminals or ground connections

fixSteps.title: Test battery
fixSteps.instruction: Test battery voltage (should be 12.4V+ with engine off). Load test the battery to check its condition.

fixSteps.title: Test charging system
fixSteps.instruction: With engine running, verify alternator output is 13.5-14.5V at the battery terminals. Check the charging system for proper regulation.

fixSteps.title: Inspect connections
fixSteps.instruction: Clean and tighten battery terminals, cable ends, and main ground connections. Check for corrosion at all power distribution points.

fixSteps.title: Check for parasitic draw
fixSteps.instruction: If the battery is good but keeps going dead, perform a parasitic draw test to identify any module or circuit drawing excessive current with the vehicle off.

faq.q: Can a weak battery cause multiple electrical problems?
faq.a: Yes. A weak battery or poor charging system can cause a cascade of electrical issues and trigger codes in multiple modules, not just the BCM.

faq.q: How long does a car battery typically last?
faq.a: Most car batteries last 3-5 years depending on climate, usage, and maintenance. Hot climates tend to shorten battery life.

faq.q: Should I replace the battery or the alternator?
faq.a: Test both. If the battery fails a load test, replace it first. If the battery is good but the alternator output is low or erratic, the alternator needs attention.

title: Driver Seat Position Sensor Circuit
shortDescription: Malfunction in the driver seat position sensor used by the SRS system.
fullDescription: Code B1015 indicates a fault in the driver seat position sensor circuit. The SRS system uses this sensor to determine the driver's distance from the steering wheel to adjust airbag deployment force. A fault in this sensor can cause the airbag to deploy with incorrect force for the driver's seating position.
safetyImpact: Airbag deployment force may not be optimized for the driver's seating position, potentially causing more harm in close-proximity deployment.

causes.cause: Corroded connector at seat track sensor

fixSteps.title: Read codes
fixSteps.instruction: Scan SRS system for codes. Check if the seat position sensor code is the only fault.

fixSteps.title: Inspect seat track sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Slide the driver seat fully forward and back while monitoring sensor output with a scanner. Check for intermittent readings or dropouts.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring and connector
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring harness under the driver seat and the connector at the seat track sensor for damage, pinching, or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the seat track position sensor if faulty. Clear codes and verify proper operation across full seat travel range.

faq.q: Does this affect how my seat moves?
faq.a: No. The seat position sensor only reports position to the SRS system. The power seat motors and controls are separate systems.

faq.q: Why does the SRS system need to know seat position?
faq.a: The system adjusts airbag deployment force based on how close the driver is to the steering wheel. A close-seated driver receives a gentler deployment to prevent injury from the airbag itself.

faq.q: Can debris in the seat track cause this code?
faq.a: In some cases, yes. Debris or objects caught in the seat track can interfere with the position sensor. Clean the seat tracks as a first step.

title: Driver Seat Position Sensor Range/Performance
shortDescription: Driver seat position sensor signal is out of expected range or performance parameters.
fullDescription: Code B1016 indicates that the driver seat position sensor is providing readings outside the expected range or the signal performance is degraded. Unlike a circuit fault, this code suggests the sensor is communicating but providing incorrect data. This may be due to sensor calibration issues, mechanical misalignment, or gradual sensor degradation.
safetyImpact: SRS system may not accurately determine driver position, affecting airbag deployment optimization.

causes.cause: Seat position sensor out of calibration
Sensor mechanical misalignment on seat track
Intermittent wiring connection causing signal fluctuation

fixSteps.title: Monitor sensor data
fixSteps.instruction: Using a diagnostic scanner, monitor the seat position sensor output in real-time while moving the seat through its full travel range. Look for erratic, stuck, or out-of-range values.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the physical mounting and alignment of the seat position sensor on the seat track. Ensure it has not shifted or come loose.

fixSteps.title: Recalibrate sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Some vehicles allow seat position sensor recalibration through the diagnostic tool. Perform the calibration procedure per manufacturer instructions.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If calibration does not resolve the issue, replace the sensor. Perform the calibration procedure after installation.

faq.q: What is the difference between B1015 and B1016?
faq.a: B1015 indicates a circuit fault (open, short, no signal), while B1016 indicates the circuit is working but the sensor readings are out of the expected range or performance parameters.

faq.q: Can I recalibrate the sensor myself?
faq.a: Recalibration typically requires a professional diagnostic tool with manufacturer-specific software. Consumer-grade scanners usually cannot perform this procedure.

faq.q: How long do seat position sensors last?
faq.a: They are generally reliable for the life of the vehicle, but can fail due to wear, contamination, or connector issues, especially in vehicles with high mileage.

title: Driver Door Module Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication fault between the driver door module and the body control module.
fullDescription: Code B1047 indicates a communication error between the Driver Door Module (DDM) and the Body Control Module. The DDM controls and monitors driver door functions including the window, mirror, door lock, and switches. A communication fault can disable multiple driver door convenience features.
safetyImpact: Driver door lock, window, and mirror functions may be affected. Power lock failure can be a security concern.

causes.cause: Damaged wiring in driver door jamb flex area
Communication bus fault on door circuit

symptoms: Driver window inoperative from master switch
Driver door lock not responding to remote or switch
Multiple driver door features failing simultaneously

fixSteps.title: Scan for codes
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a full vehicle scan. Check for communication codes in both the BCM and DDM. Check for related U-codes.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door jamb wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Open the driver door and inspect the wiring harness that passes through the door jamb boot. This flex area is the most common failure point.

fixSteps.title: Test wiring continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Check continuity on the communication bus wires from the DDM through the door jamb to the BCM. Flex the harness while testing to find intermittent breaks.

fixSteps.title: Repair wiring or replace module
fixSteps.instruction: Repair broken wires in the door jamb harness, or replace the DDM if the module itself has failed. Clear codes and test all door functions.

faq.q: Why do door wires break so often?
faq.a: The wiring harness in the door jamb flexes every time the door opens and closes. Over thousands of cycles, individual wires can fatigue and break inside the insulation.

faq.q: Will my door still lock manually?
faq.a: Yes. The physical door lock button or key cylinder should still work even if the electronic module has lost communication.

faq.q: Can I use the passenger switches if the driver door module fails?
faq.a: Possibly. If the passenger door has its own module, those switches may still function. However, the master window/lock controls on the driver door will not work.

title: Climate Control System Communication Fault
shortDescription: Communication error between the climate control module and vehicle network.
fullDescription: Code B1200 indicates a communication fault involving the climate control (HVAC) module. The module has lost communication with the vehicle network or is not receiving expected data from other modules it depends on (such as ambient temperature, engine coolant temperature, or vehicle speed). This can result in the climate control system defaulting to a fail-safe mode or becoming non-functional.
safetyImpact: Loss of climate control can affect defogging capability, which is a safety concern in cold or wet weather.
symptoms: Climate control display blank or frozen
HVAC system stuck in one mode
No response from climate control buttons
Blower fan running at one speed or not at all

fixSteps.title: Full system scan
fixSteps.instruction: Scan all modules for codes. Communication faults often originate from a single point affecting multiple modules.

fixSteps.title: Check HVAC module connector
fixSteps.instruction: Access the HVAC module (usually behind the center dashboard) and inspect its electrical connector for corrosion, loose pins, or damage.

fixSteps.title: Test communication bus
fixSteps.instruction: Check the communication bus (LIN or CAN) voltage and activity at the HVAC module connector. Verify proper bus termination.

fixSteps.title: Replace module or repair wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the climate control module if it has failed internally, or repair the communication bus wiring. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: Will my heater still work?
faq.a: In fail-safe mode, the HVAC system may provide limited heating but without automatic temperature control or mode selection.

faq.q: Can I manually control the defrost?
faq.a: If the control module has lost communication, the defrost button may not respond. This is a safety concern in cold or wet weather and should be repaired promptly.

faq.q: Is this related to refrigerant level?
faq.a: No. This code is a communication/electronic fault, not a refrigerant or mechanical issue with the A/C system.

title: Anti-Theft System Transponder Key Not Recognized
shortDescription: The immobilizer system cannot recognize or authenticate the transponder key.
fullDescription: Code B1213 indicates that the vehicle's anti-theft immobilizer system cannot read or authenticate the transponder chip in the ignition key. The immobilizer must verify the key's transponder code before allowing the engine to start. This code can be set when using a non-programmed key, a damaged key, or when the immobilizer system has a fault.
safetyImpact: Vehicle may not start, leaving the driver stranded. Not a direct safety hazard but can create unsafe situations.

causes.cause: Key transponder chip damaged or weakened
Immobilizer antenna ring around ignition faulty
Key not properly programmed to vehicle

symptoms: Engine cranks but will not start
Security or immobilizer warning light on

fixSteps.title: Try backup key
fixSteps.instruction: Attempt to start the vehicle with the spare/backup key. If the spare key works, the primary key's transponder is likely faulty.

fixSteps.title: Scan immobilizer system
fixSteps.instruction: Read codes from the immobilizer/security module. Check if the key ID is being read at all or if authentication is failing.

fixSteps.title: Check antenna ring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the immobilizer antenna ring around the ignition cylinder. Check for damage and measure its resistance/output.

fixSteps.title: Reprogram or replace key
fixSteps.instruction: Have a new key cut and programmed, or reprogram the existing key using a locksmith or dealer tool. If the antenna ring or module is faulty, replace it.

faq.q: Can I bypass the immobilizer?
faq.a: Bypassing the immobilizer is not recommended as it disables a key anti-theft feature and may also be illegal in some jurisdictions. Proper repair is the correct approach.

faq.q: Why did my key suddenly stop working?
faq.a: Transponder chips can fail due to physical damage (dropping the key), battery depletion (in some key types), or simply wear out over time.

faq.q: Can a locksmith program a new key?
faq.a: Yes. Many automotive locksmiths have the equipment to cut and program transponder keys, often at lower cost than a dealership.

title: Battery Voltage Low
shortDescription: Vehicle battery voltage is below the acceptable operating threshold.
fullDescription: Code B1318 indicates that the vehicle's electrical system has detected battery voltage below the acceptable minimum threshold. This is a general body system code that can be set by the BCM, PCM, or other modules when system voltage drops below approximately 9-10 volts. Persistent low voltage affects the operation of all electronic modules and can cause a cascade of other fault codes.
safetyImpact: Low voltage can cause erratic operation of safety systems including lighting, ABS, power steering, and SRS.

causes.cause: Excessive electrical load or parasitic draw

fixSteps.title: Test battery
fixSteps.instruction: Measure battery resting voltage (engine off, 12.4V+ expected). Perform a load test or conductance test to assess battery health.

fixSteps.title: Test alternator
fixSteps.instruction: With the engine running, measure voltage at the battery (13.5-14.5V expected). Test alternator output current under load.

fixSteps.title: Clean connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove, clean, and tighten all battery terminal connections and main ground straps. Apply dielectric grease to prevent future corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace battery or alternator
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the battery if it fails testing, or the alternator if output is low. After replacement, clear all codes and verify they do not return.

faq.q: Can cold weather cause this code?
faq.a: Yes. Cold temperatures reduce battery capacity. A marginal battery may trigger low voltage codes in cold weather that would not appear in warmer conditions.

faq.q: Should I replace the battery or alternator first?
faq.a: Always test the battery first. A failed battery can make the alternator appear to be faulty because the alternator is working harder to charge a bad battery.

faq.q: Can aftermarket accessories cause low voltage?
faq.a: Yes. Aftermarket stereo systems, lighting, winches, and other high-draw accessories can overload the charging system and cause low voltage conditions.

title: BCM General Input/Output Fault
shortDescription: General input or output circuit fault detected by the body control module.
fullDescription: Code B1325 indicates a general input or output fault detected by the Body Control Module. The BCM has identified that one or more of its input circuits (sensors, switches) or output circuits (relays, actuators) is not functioning correctly. This is a catch-all code that may require further diagnosis to identify the specific circuit at fault.
safetyImpact: Impact depends on which specific I/O circuit is affected. Could range from minor convenience feature loss to lighting issues.

causes.cause: Faulty sensor or switch providing incorrect input
Failed relay or actuator on output circuit
Wiring fault on a BCM-controlled circuit
BCM pin or internal circuit issue

symptoms: One or more body electrical features malfunctioning
Specific symptom depends on affected circuit

fixSteps.title: Identify affected circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Scan for all codes and freeze frame data. Cross-reference the code sub-type (if available) with the wiring diagram to identify which specific I/O circuit is affected.

fixSteps.title: Test the specific circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Once identified, test the specific sensor, switch, relay, or actuator for proper operation. Check for correct voltage and resistance.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the wiring for the affected circuit from the BCM to the component. Look for damage, chafing, or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Repair or replace
fixSteps.instruction: Repair wiring or replace the faulty component. Clear codes and verify the feature operates correctly.

faq.q: How do I know which circuit is affected?
faq.a: The freeze frame data and sub-codes (if available) can help narrow down the circuit. Also, note which features are malfunctioning, as this points to the affected BCM output or input.

faq.q: Can a blown fuse cause this code?
faq.a: Yes. If a fuse protecting a BCM output circuit blows, the BCM may detect the loss of output and set this code. Check all related fuses.

faq.q: Is this a common code?
faq.a: B1325 is relatively common because it can be triggered by many different I/O circuit issues. The key is determining which specific circuit is at fault.

title: Left Headlamp Low Beam Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: The left low beam headlamp circuit has an electrical fault.
fullDescription:
Code B0033 indicates the body control module (BCM) has detected a malfunction in the left low beam headlamp circuit. This could be caused by an open circuit, short to ground, or short to battery voltage in the headlamp wiring harness.

The BCM monitors current draw on headlamp circuits and compares it to expected values. When the current is out of specification, this code is set. The left low beam headlamp may not illuminate or may flicker intermittently.

Driving at night without a functioning low beam headlamp is dangerous and typically illegal. This code should be addressed promptly for safety and legal compliance.
safetyImpact: Reduced nighttime visibility due to non-functional left low beam headlamp, increasing collision risk.

causes.cause: Burned out left low beam headlamp bulb
Corroded or damaged headlamp bulb socket
Broken or chafed wiring in headlamp circuit
Failed headlamp relay or BCM output driver

symptoms: Left low beam headlamp not illuminating

fixSteps.title: Check the bulb
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the left low beam headlamp bulb. Remove it and check the filament or LED element for damage. Replace if burned out.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the socket and connector
fixSteps.instruction: Check the headlamp bulb socket for corrosion, melting, or loose terminals. Clean or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Test the circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to check for voltage at the headlamp connector with the headlamps turned on. Check ground circuit continuity.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring harness
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the wiring from the BCM to the headlamp looking for chafing, breaks, or shorts. Repair as needed and clear codes.

faq.q: Can I drive with only one low beam working?
faq.a: While the car will run, driving with one low beam is unsafe and illegal in most jurisdictions. Fix the issue before driving at night.

faq.q: Do I need to replace bulbs in pairs?
faq.a: It is recommended to replace both low beam bulbs at the same time since the other may fail soon if they were installed together.

title: Right Headlamp Low Beam Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: The right low beam headlamp circuit has an electrical fault.
fullDescription:
Code B0034 indicates the BCM has detected a malfunction in the right low beam headlamp circuit. The system monitors current flow through the headlamp circuit and triggers this code when values fall outside normal range.

Common causes include a burned-out bulb, corroded socket, or wiring damage. The BCM continuously monitors headlamp circuits and will set this code if the expected load is not detected on the right low beam circuit.

This code affects nighttime driving safety and should be repaired promptly. Most states require both headlamps to function for legal road use.
safetyImpact: Right low beam headlamp not functioning, reducing nighttime visibility and potentially violating traffic laws.

causes.cause: Burned out right low beam bulb
Faulty headlamp relay or BCM driver

fixSteps.title: Inspect bulb
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and inspect the right low beam bulb for a broken filament or LED element failure. Replace if defective.

fixSteps.title: Check socket condition
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the headlamp socket for melting, corrosion, or loose pin connections. Clean contacts or replace the socket.

fixSteps.title: Verify circuit voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test for battery voltage at the headlamp connector with lights on. Verify ground integrity.

fixSteps.title: Repair wiring
fixSteps.instruction: If voltage is absent, trace the circuit back to the BCM or relay, checking for opens or shorts. Repair damaged wiring.

faq.q: Why does my headlamp keep burning out?
faq.a: Frequent bulb failure can be caused by a corroded socket creating high resistance, voltage spikes, or touching halogen bulbs with bare hands during installation (oil causes hot spots).

faq.q: Could the BCM cause this code?
faq.a: Yes, though rare. If the BCM output driver fails, it cannot provide power to the headlamp. This would require BCM diagnosis by a professional.

title: Left Headlamp High Beam Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: The left high beam headlamp circuit has an electrical fault.
fullDescription:
Code B0035 is stored when the BCM detects an abnormal condition in the left high beam headlamp circuit. The module monitors the circuit for proper current draw when high beams are commanded on.

The fault could be a simple burned-out bulb or a more complex wiring issue. On vehicles with separate high and low beam bulbs, the high beam filament or bulb may have failed independently. On dual-filament bulbs, the high beam filament may burn out while the low beam remains functional.

While high beams are not used constantly, they are critical for safe driving on unlit rural roads and highways. This code should be addressed to maintain full lighting capability.
safetyImpact: Reduced maximum visibility range on dark roads when high beams are needed.

causes.cause: Burned out high beam bulb or filament
Wiring fault in high beam circuit

symptoms: Left high beam does not illuminate when selected
Dashboard high beam indicator may not light up on left side
Headlamp warning message on instrument cluster

fixSteps.title: Test bulb
fixSteps.instruction: Check the left high beam bulb by swapping it with the right side or installing a known-good bulb. Replace if faulty.

fixSteps.title: Inspect connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Check the headlamp connector and any intermediate connectors for corrosion, damage, or loose terminals.

fixSteps.title: Test circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Measure voltage at the high beam connector when high beams are commanded on. Check ground continuity. If no voltage, trace back to the relay or BCM.

fixSteps.title: Check multifunction switch
fixSteps.instruction: If voltage is present at BCM output but not at connector, check the high beam relay and multifunction switch operation.

faq.q: Is it safe to drive without high beams?
faq.a: High beams are essential for rural and unlit road driving. While you can drive in lit areas, repair the issue before any nighttime highway or rural driving.

faq.q: Could a bad relay cause this?
faq.a: Yes. The high beam relay supplies power to both high beam circuits. If it fails intermittently, it may affect only one side due to connector resistance differences.

title: Left Front Turn Signal Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: The left front turn signal circuit is not operating correctly.
fullDescription:
Code B0040 indicates the BCM has detected a fault in the left front turn signal lamp circuit. The BCM monitors turn signal circuits for correct current draw and flash rate. When the current is too low (open circuit/burned bulb) or too high (short circuit), this code is set.

A common symptom is a rapid flash rate (hyperflash) on the left turn signal, which indicates reduced load on the circuit, usually from a burned-out bulb. The rapid flash is designed as a driver alert that a bulb has failed.

Turn signals are legally required safety equipment. Operating a vehicle without functional turn signals creates a hazard for other drivers and may result in traffic citations.
safetyImpact: Inability to signal left turns to other drivers increases accident risk significantly.

causes.cause: Burned out left front turn signal bulb
Wiring damage from road debris or corrosion

symptoms: Left front turn signal not flashing
Rapid flash rate (hyperflash) when left turn signal is activated
Turn signal indicator on dashboard flashes rapidly

fixSteps.title: Replace bulb
fixSteps.instruction: Access the left front turn signal bulb, remove it, and inspect. Replace with the correct replacement bulb. Verify operation.

fixSteps.title: Clean socket
fixSteps.instruction: If the new bulb does not fix the issue, clean the bulb socket contacts and ground connection with electrical contact cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Test circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter or test light to verify power and ground at the turn signal socket with the turn signal activated.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the wiring from the socket back to the BCM, checking for breaks, chafing, or corrosion in connectors.

faq.q: Why does my turn signal flash faster than normal?
faq.a: Hyperflash (rapid flashing) occurs when the turn signal circuit has reduced electrical load, almost always from a burned-out bulb. It is a built-in warning feature.

faq.q: Can I use LED replacement bulbs?
faq.a: LED bulbs draw less current and may cause hyperflash. You may need to install a load resistor or an LED-compatible flasher module.

title: Daytime Running Lamp (DRL) Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: The daytime running lamp circuit has an electrical fault.
fullDescription:
Code B0045 is set when the BCM detects a fault in the daytime running lamp (DRL) circuit. DRLs are designed to increase vehicle visibility during daylight hours and are mandatory in many countries.

The BCM monitors the DRL circuit for proper operation. A fault may be caused by a burned-out DRL bulb, LED module failure, wiring damage, or a faulty DRL control module. On some vehicles, the DRLs use the headlamp bulbs at reduced brightness, while on others, separate dedicated DRL units are used.

While DRL failure is not as critical as headlamp failure, it reduces daytime visibility and may violate regulations in jurisdictions where DRLs are required.
safetyImpact: Reduced daytime vehicle visibility to other motorists, increasing collision risk in certain conditions.

causes.cause: Burned out or failed DRL bulb/LED module
Failed DRL control module or BCM output

fixSteps.title: Inspect DRL bulbs
fixSteps.instruction: Check DRL bulbs or LED modules for failure. Replace burned-out bulbs or failed LED units.
tools: Replacement DRL bulb or LED module

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the DRL socket and connector for corrosion or damage. Clean and secure all connections.

fixSteps.title: Test circuit power
fixSteps.instruction: Verify voltage at the DRL connector with the ignition on and vehicle in gear (DRL activation condition). Check ground circuit.

fixSteps.title: Diagnose control module
fixSteps.instruction: If power and ground are good but DRL is inoperative, test the DRL module or BCM output using a scan tool and wiring diagrams.

faq.q: Are daytime running lamps required by law?
faq.a: DRLs are mandatory in Canada, the EU, and many other countries. In the US, they are not required but are standard on most modern vehicles.

faq.q: Can I disable my DRLs?
faq.a: While technically possible, disabling DRLs may violate regulations in some jurisdictions and reduces your vehicle's visibility to others.

title: Right Rear Turn Signal Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: The right rear turn signal lamp circuit has an electrical fault.
fullDescription:
Code B0050 indicates a fault in the right rear turn signal circuit. The BCM has detected abnormal current draw or no current flow when the right turn signal is activated. This typically means the rear turn signal lamp is not functioning.

The rear turn signals are critical for communicating lane changes, turns, and hazard awareness to drivers behind you. A malfunctioning rear turn signal greatly increases the risk of rear-end collisions during turning maneuvers.

This code is often accompanied by hyperflash on the affected side. On vehicles with trailer towing capability, ensure the issue is not related to the trailer wiring harness before replacing the vehicle bulb.
safetyImpact: Other drivers cannot see right turn intentions from behind, significantly increasing rear-end collision risk.

causes.cause: Burned out right rear turn signal bulb
Corroded tail light socket or ground connection
Water intrusion into tail light assembly
Wiring damage between BCM and tail light

symptoms: Right rear turn signal not flashing
Rapid flash rate (hyperflash) on right turn signal
Dashboard turn signal indicator flashes rapidly
Possible hazard light malfunction on right side

fixSteps.title: Replace bulb
fixSteps.instruction: Access the right rear tail light assembly, remove the turn signal bulb, and replace with correct type.

fixSteps.title: Check for water intrusion
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the tail light assembly for moisture, condensation, or water damage. Replace gaskets or seal as needed.

fixSteps.title: Test ground circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Check the tail light ground connection for corrosion. Clean and ensure a solid ground to the vehicle body.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: If bulb and socket are good, trace the wiring from the tail light to the BCM for damage, especially at body panel pass-throughs.

faq.q: Can a trailer wiring issue cause this code?
faq.a: Yes. If your vehicle has a trailer wiring harness, a short or ground fault in the trailer circuit can affect the vehicle turn signal circuit and set this code.

faq.q: Why does my tail light assembly have moisture inside?
faq.a: A cracked lens, deteriorated gasket, or improperly sealed assembly allows water entry, which corrodes sockets and grounds, leading to bulb circuit failures.

title: Brake Lamp Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: One or more brake lamp circuits have an electrical fault.
fullDescription:
Code B0065 indicates the BCM has detected a malfunction in a brake lamp circuit. The brake lamps are monitored by the BCM for proper current draw when the brake pedal is pressed. An open circuit, short circuit, or incorrect current draw will trigger this code.

Brake lamps are arguably the most critical exterior lighting on a vehicle. They alert following drivers that you are decelerating or stopped. A non-functional brake lamp dramatically increases the risk of rear-end collisions.

This code should be treated as an urgent safety concern. Many jurisdictions allow law enforcement to stop and cite vehicles with non-functional brake lamps.
safetyImpact: Following drivers cannot see braking, creating extreme rear-end collision danger.

causes.cause: Corroded tail light socket or ground
Wiring fault in brake lamp circuit

symptoms: One or more brake lights not illuminating when pedal is pressed
Third brake light may still work while lower lamps are out

fixSteps.title: Check all brake lamps
fixSteps.instruction: Have someone press the brake pedal while you inspect all brake lamps (left, right, and center high-mount). Identify which are not working.

fixSteps.title: Replace bulbs
fixSteps.instruction: Replace any burned-out brake lamp bulbs. Dual-filament bulbs may have only the brake filament burned out while the tail light filament works.

fixSteps.title: Test brake light switch
fixSteps.instruction: If no brake lamps work, check the brake light switch located at the top of the brake pedal. Test for voltage output when the pedal is pressed.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring and grounds
fixSteps.instruction: Check tail light grounds and wiring continuity from the brake light switch through the BCM to each brake lamp socket.

faq.q: Can a bad brake light switch cause this code?
faq.a: Yes. The brake light switch provides the signal to illuminate all brake lamps. If it fails, none of the brake lamps will work.

faq.q: My third brake light works but the lower ones do not. Why?
faq.a: The third brake light (CHMSL) is often on a separate circuit. Lower brake lamps share a different power feed and ground circuit, so a separate fault can affect only those.

title: Fog Lamp Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: The fog lamp circuit has an electrical fault preventing proper operation.
fullDescription:
